prompt: Use the attached benchmark markdown extraction and counting rules to produce a benchmark length profile. Segment the source into front-page, toc/index, body, detailed financials, and disclaimer blocks; then report raw_md_chars, clean_full_report_chars, transferable_body_chars, chapter counts, chapter weights, and ratio-derived total budgets. Do not rely on fixed default word counts.
expected_output: The output should behave like a reusable benchmark profile: separate raw from clean from transferable counts, explain what is removed from transferable scope, derive chapter weights from the transferable body, and compute short / medium / full budgets from ratio bands rather than from legacy fixed presets.
source_files: materials/广发证券-【广发计算机&海外】MINIMAX-WP（00100.HK）-Born-Global的稀缺全模态大模型公司-刘雪峰-公司深度研究-增持-2026-02-10-3.md

assertions.text: Segments the source into front-page, toc/index, body, detailed financials, and disclaimer or research-team buckets rather than treating the markdown extraction as one undifferentiated block.
assertions.grading_hint: Passing requires all major buckets or an equivalent segmentation with the same meaning.

assertions.text: Reports raw_md_chars, clean_full_report_chars, and transferable_body_chars as three distinct counts.
assertions.grading_hint: Fail if the output reports only one number or collapses clean and transferable into the same unlabeled bucket.

assertions.text: Explains which listed-equity blocks are removed from transferable scope instead of keeping rating, target-price, or detailed forecast shells inside the transferable budget.
assertions.grading_hint: Passing outputs should explicitly mention stock-rating or valuation-shell removals, not just say some parts are excluded.

assertions.text: Derives chapter counts and chapter weights from the transferable body rather than from intuition or legacy chapter presets.
assertions.grading_hint: Passing outputs should show chapter-level derived counts or weights, not only broad chapter names.

assertions.text: Computes short, medium, and full total budgets from ratio bands rather than from the old fixed 1500 to 8000 budget system.
assertions.grading_hint: Fail if the output falls back to the legacy fixed presets or omits ratio-based budget derivation.

human_review_gates: Does the profile look reusable as a benchmark-calibration asset rather than a one-off note?
Does the transferable count feel grounded in the report body instead of inflated by indexes and disclaimers?
Would this profile let a later run derive budgets without manual guessing?
